title: A decade of progress in small-cell lung cancer (2015-2025): a narrative review.
abstract: Small-cell lung cancer (SCLC) accounts for 13-15% of lung-cancer diagnoses but nearly one quarter of lung-cancer-related deaths. Until 2015, the combination of platinum-etoposide chemotherapy, thoracic radiotherapy and prophylactic cranial irradiation (PCI) had kept median overall survival broadly stable at ≈ 12 months in extensive-stage (ES-SCLC) and ≈ 24 months in limited-stage (LS-SCLC) disease. We summarise the key therapeutic advances of the last decade.
